items.title: Widget sync exposes raw err.message in alert
items.description: Line 105 in script.js: `alert("위젯 동기화 오류: " + err.message)` — internal bridge error message directly exposed to user. Should show a friendly message instead.
steps: Open the app in browser (guest mode is fine)
Inspect script.js around the WidgetBridge.setSelectedCalendar().catch() block
Verify the alert would show raw err.message if triggered

items.title: OAuth login exposes raw error.message in alert
items.description: Lines 244 and 246 in script.js: signIn() catches errors with `alert("로그인 오류: " + error.message)` and `alert("로그인 시스템 오류: " + e.message)`. These can expose internal Supabase/OAuth error details to users.
steps: Open script.js and find the signIn() function
Check the catch blocks at lines 244 and 246
Verify that error.message is passed directly to alert without sanitization

items.title: Early return blocks app init when Supabase client is null
items.description: Line 781 in script.js: `if (!DataManager.client) { return; }` returns early from the entire DOMContentLoaded handler. This skips session check, guest mode setup, AND initializeCalendar() — so in local dev with empty config.js credentials, the app renders an empty unusable state instead of correctly falling back to guest mode.
steps: Open docs/index.html locally (or check that config.js has empty credentials)
Observe whether the calendar renders and the guest mode loads
Check browser console for the Supabase not initialized warning

items.title: Unguarded JSON.parse in updateWidgetCalendar fallback
items.description: Line 68 in script.js: inside updateWidgetCalendar(), there is a bare `JSON.parse(stored)` without try-catch when falling back to re-read guest_calendars from localStorage. If stored value is corrupted, this will throw an uncaught exception and crash the function silently.
steps: In script.js locate the updateWidgetCalendar() function
Find the block around line 65-69 that parses guest_calendars as a last-resort fallback
Verify JSON.parse is called without try-catch protection

items.title: Holiday data hardcoded for 2026 only
items.description: The getHolidaysForWeek() function in CalendarUtils (lines 654-673) contains only 2026 holidays. In 2027 and beyond, no holidays will be displayed on the calendar. The 2025 holidays are also missing.
steps: Navigate the calendar to December 2025 — verify 성탄절 is NOT shown (it should be)
Navigate to January 2027 — verify 신정 is NOT shown (it should be)
Navigate to January 2026 — verify 신정 IS shown

items.title: Redundant WidgetBridge null check after guard clause
items.description: Lines 35-41 in updateWidgetCalendar(): `if (!WidgetBridge) { return; }` is followed immediately by `if (WidgetBridge) { ... }`. The inner `if (WidgetBridge)` is always true at that point — this is dead code that adds unnecessary nesting.
steps: Open script.js and find updateWidgetCalendar()
Observe lines 35-41: first a guard `if (!WidgetBridge) return`, then immediately `if (WidgetBridge) {`
Confirm the inner if-check is always true (since the guard already handled the false case)

items.title: deleteSchedule catch block swallows the actual error
items.description: Line 1377 in script.js: the `deleteSchedule` UI handler catches errors but only does `alert("삭제 실패")` without including the actual error message. When delete fails, the user gets no actionable information.
steps: Open script.js and locate the deleteSchedule function (UI handler, around line 1369)
Examine the catch block — it calls alert('삭제 실패') with no error details
Compare with other error handlers in the file which do include e.message

items.title: HTML lang attribute is 'en' but UI is Korean
items.description: docs/index.html line 2: `<html lang="en">` — the entire UI, buttons, labels, and modals are written in Korean. Screen readers and browser translation tools will incorrectly identify the page language.
steps: Open docs/index.html
Inspect the lang attribute on the <html> element
Verify all visible text in the app is in Korean

items.title: Calendar selected indicator uses ASCII '(V)' instead of checkmark
items.description: Line 936 in script.js: `cal.title + (cal.id === DataManager.currentCalendarId ? ' (V)' : '')` — uses a parenthesized letter V as a selected indicator. Should use a proper Unicode checkmark like '✓' or '✔'.
steps: Open the app in a browser (guest mode)
Open the calendar drawer (menu icon)
Observe the active calendar label — it shows '(V)' suffix instead of a checkmark symbol

items.title: Excessive console.log statements left in production code
items.description: script.js contains dozens of console.log calls throughout production code paths (e.g., lines 93, 94, 119, 134, 137, 179, 199, 300, 309, etc.). These clutter the browser console and may expose internal state information in production.
steps: Open browser developer tools console
Load the app in guest mode
Observe the number of log statements appearing during normal app load and interaction
